lever: the UNDIRECTED native _raw_is_eulerian kernel does O(E) work and was 18-30x slower than nx on dense graphs (K301: 1.65ms vs 0.06ms). nx's test is trivial: all degrees even AND connected. Replace the undirected _raw call with the direct check using fnx's O(V) degree view + native is_connected, matching nx's short-circuit order (degrees first). DIRECTED keeps the native kernel (already fast: 6.6x faster than nx).

parity.coverage: complete/cycle/path (eulerian + not), directed+undirected, self-loops (delegated path), empty/single/null, disconnected, 40 random gnp directed+undirected

dicycle_dir.note: unchanged, native kernel kept; 6.6x faster than nx (vs_nx 0.15)

headline: is_eulerian undirected: native kernel was 18-30x slower than nx on dense graphs -> 7.4-18.6x self-speedup via the trivial degree-parity + is_connected check, byte-exact. Directed kept on the fast native kernel. Pure Python, no rebuild.
residual: undirected vs-nx 1.07-3.14x (grows with density) = the substrate is_connected + DegreeView O(V) cost on dense graphs; huge improvement from 18-30x. is_connected dense substrate is a separate native-kernel gap worth a follow-up.
